canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, human subject studies represent a versatile category of measurement methods that involve human participants in evaluating AI systems or collecting data. One common application is for quality assurance, where participants are asked to assess the "reliability and quality" of generated test cases and model responses. These studies are also operationalized as a "data collection procedure" to establish human performance baselines, for example by having participants perform a task to compute "humans’ raw accuracy". A prevalent use involves deploying surveys to gather perspectives from specific populations, such as surveying community members to identify "harmful model behaviours" or understanding "adoption patterns, use cases, and challenges" among users with visual impairments. The methodologies described vary, including "mixed-methods survey[s]" with "open-ended questions and Likert scale ratings" and procedures where participants submit guesses after viewing a sequence of clues.
papers: Deep Temporal Reasoning in Video Language Models: A Cross-Linguistic Evaluation of Action Duration and Completion through Perfect Times
Dehumanizing Machines: Mitigating Anthropomorphic Behaviors in Text Generation Systems
ReSCORE: Label-free Iterative Retriever Training for Multi-hop Question Answering with Relevance-Consistency Supervision
ZIPA: A family of efficient models for multilingual phone recognition

definitions.text: A human evaluation method used for quality assurance of the framework, involving participants assessing the reliability and quality of generated test cases and model responses.
definitions.paper: ReSCORE: Label-free Iterative Retriever Training for Multi-hop Question Answering with Relevance-Consistency Supervision

definitions.text: A survey of 120 TGNB community members in the USA to gather broad perspectives on harmful model behaviours and ideal model responses.
definitions.paper: Deep Temporal Reasoning in Video Language Models: A Cross-Linguistic Evaluation of Action Duration and Completion through Perfect Times

definitions.text: A data collection procedure where human participants view a sequence of clues for held-out questions and submit a guess after each clue, also indicating whether they would choose to 'buzz' at that point.
definitions.paper: ZIPA: A family of efficient models for multilingual phone recognition

definitions.text: A mixed-methods survey with open-ended questions and Likert scale ratings administered to 106 participants with visual impairments to identify adoption patterns, use cases, and challenges with AI visual assistants.
definitions.paper: Dehumanizing Machines: Mitigating Anthropomorphic Behaviors in Text Generation Systems
